title: My Proven AI Agent Formula Explained

extracted.hook: These are the five steps I always use when building AI agents, and they've helped me generate over $240,000 in the past 6 months.
extracted.credential_drop: "These are the five steps I always use when building AI agents, and they've helped me generate over $240,000 in the past 6 months." (early)
transitions: "Okay. The second step is actually identifying high ROI opportunities."
"Now that we've identified the process, the third step is process mapping."
"Step number four is a really, really important one, which is workflows versus AI agents."
"The fifth step is about PC, proof of concept, and guardrails. So there are three things that I like to say here."
extracted.cta: "if this sounds like something that you want to dive into and it interests you, then definitely check out my paid course and community using the link down in the description."
analogies: "And so diving into vector databases, it's definitely important to understand the way that we take a document, we chunk it up, we run it through an embedded model and those chunks are placed into the vector database based on the actual meaning of the words in the chunk."

"So here's an example. Alice is a 25-year-old student. We have name Alice, age 25, is student true. And here's an example where we have an array. We have a shopping list with apples, bananas, and milk. And over here, we have the shopping list array with apples, bananas, and milk."
"And so basically, our job here in the process mapping step is to build out the instruction manual."
curiosity_gaps: "So, that's going to do it for today's video. I hope you guys enjoyed and I hope you learned something new. If you did, please give it a like. Definitely helps me out a ton. And as always, I appreciate you guys sticking around to the end of the video."
"And this is exactly why I created the course from 10 hours to 10 seconds, which is basically the exact methodology that I just covered with you guys, but in much more detail."
extracted.vulnerability: "I see way too many people trying to build these systems when they don't understand how to read JSON." (early)

accelerate: When explaining the foundational elements of JSON and APIs
slow: During detailed explanations like vector databases and large language models
